title: Use an Image Volume With a Pod — UsesubPath(orsubPathExpr)
text:
It is possible to utilize
subPath
or
subPathExpr
from Kubernetes v1.33 when using the image volume feature.
apiVersion
:
v1
kind
:
Pod
metadata
:
name
:
image-volume
spec
:
containers
:
-
name
:
shell
command
:
[
"sleep"
,
"infinity"
]
image
:
debian
volumeMounts
:
-
name
:
volume
mountPath
:
/volume
subPath
:
dir
volumes
:
-
name
:
volume
image
:
reference
:
quay.io/crio/artifact:v2
pullPolicy
:
IfNotPresent
Create the pod on your cluster:
kubectl apply -f https://k8s.io/examples/pods/image-volumes-subpath.yaml
Attach to the container:
kubectl
exec
image-volume -it -- bash
Check the content of the file from the
dir
sub path in the volume:
cat /volume/file
The output is similar to:
1
Create the pod on your cluster:
kubectl apply -f https://k8s.io/examples/pods/image-volumes-subpath.yaml
Attach to the container:
kubectl
exec
image-volume -it -- bash
Check the content of the file from the
dir
sub path in the volume:
cat /volume/file
The output is similar to:
1
